title: The Bombay Reorganisation Act, 1960
full_text: The Bombay Reorganisation Act, 1960 मुम्‍बई पुनर्गठन अधिनियम,1960 Rules Regulations Notifications Orders Circulars Ordinance Statutes SectionsScheduleAnnexureAppendixFormsActdetails Act ID: 196011 Act Number: 11 Enactment Date: 1960-04-25 Act Year: 1960 Short Title: The Bombay Reorganisation Act, 1960 Hindi Title: मुम्‍बई पुनर्गठन अधिनियम,1960 Long Title: An Act to provide for the reorganisation of the State of Bombay and for matters connected therewith. Short title. Section 2. Definitions. Section 3. Formation of Gujarat State. Section 4. Amendment of the First Schedule to the Constitution. Section 5. Saving powers of State Government. Section 6. Amendment of the Fourth Schedule to the Constitution. Section 7. Allocation of sitting members. Section 8. Bye-elections to fill vacancies. Section 9. Term of office. Section 10. Representation in the House of the People. Section 11. Delimitation of parliamentary constituencies. Section 12. Provision as to sitting members. Section 13. Strength of Legislative Assemblies. Section 14. Delimitation of assembly constituencies. Section 15. Allocation of members. Section 16. Duration of Legislative Assemblies. Section 17. Speakers and Deputy Speakers. Section 18. Savings. Section 41. Permanent Bench of Bombay High Court at Nagpur. Section 42. Authorisation of expenditure of Gujarat State. Section 43. Reports relating to accounts of Bombay State. Section 44. Special provision relating to retrenchment compensation in certain cases. Section 78. Special provision as to income-tax. Section 79. Continuance of facilities in certain State institutions. Section 80. Provisions relating to All-India Services. Section 81. Provisions relating to other services. Section 82. Provisions as to continuance of officers in same post. Section 83. Amendment of Act 37 of 1956. Section 87. Territorial extent of law’s. Section 88. Power to adapt laws. Section 89. Power to construe laws. Section 90. Power to name authorities, etc., for exercising statutory functions. Section 91. Legal proceedings. Section 92. Transfer of pending proceedings. Section 93. Right of pleaders to practise in certain cases. Section 94. Effect of provisions of the Act inconsistent with other laws. Section 95. Power to remove difficulties. Section 96. Power to make rules.
